notes: Opportunity openings:plaintiff. Inspected get_case and list_evidence; no newly submitted evidence beyond five case-packet files. Read all five packet files and stat metadata. Decisive elements: qualifying announcement by Clavicular/representative, content that he and a partner are expecting a baby through pregnancy, within market window, credible rather than joke/stunt. Record proves: ev_d15 market rule and final Yes/closed/resolved status; ev_f8 and ev_a72 preserve Polymarket/Gamma leads but note no original clip/statement; ev_795 secondary reporting includes Forbes account that dispute involved streamer telling audience he had gotten a girl pregnant, while ToI/HT/Tribune frame evidence as rumor/contest/stunt/no confirmed pregnancy; ev_045 lists unresolved gaps including missing original clip/VOD/transcript, Polymarket clarification, and UMA records. Native investigation performed before opening with web queries for Clavicular pregnancy announcement, X posts, and exact livestream phrases; search found same major secondary articles and social leads, plus Polymarket X and X/trending lead. Adverse check: Times of India states no confirmed girlfriend, medical update, statement, or video confirming pregnancy; HT/Tribune treat remarks as pregnancy contest/wanting children, not existing partner pregnancy. Opening will be record-bound and forward-looking: plaintiff argues the case turns on original statement/source chain and credibility, not proof of actual birth/pregnancy; acknowledges gaps and planned evidence targets. Durable notes written to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Opportunity openings:defendant. Inspected wait_for_opportunity prompt, current record via get_case, and evidence list. Record has only five initial case-packet files plus plaintiff opening; no party-submitted evidence. Read all packet files by read_evidence_range. Key record facts: ev_d15f364ccbef_market-page proves Polymarket rule language and final platform Yes state with proposed/disputed sequence; ev_f8cab9c3c684_primary-evidence states preserved primary/platform evidence proves contract wording and platform final state only, not the underlying qualifying announcement; ev_a72d9da121a5_official-source-record says no preserved Clavicular/representative primary statement exists in the packet and identifies two categories of secondary claims; ev_795651076696_secondary-reporting contains conflicting secondary reporting (Forbes favorable to plaintiff framing; Times of India/Hindustan Times/Express Tribune limiting/adverse); ev_045c38eca93e_unresolved-record-gaps enumerates missing VOD/clip/transcript/representative statement, missing platform clarification, missing UMA transaction-level records, and undefined credible standard. Native investigation: web searched targeted queries for Clavicular pregnancy Polymarket and exact phrase 'he had gotten a girl pregnant'; found public leads including Polymarket Analytics/CryptoRank mirrors, Forbes article, Binance Square summary, Lines, X leads, Popular Information. Official Gamma API curl to https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/events?slug=clavicular-pregnancy-in-2026 returned same rule language and current closed/active fields, updated 2026-06-30T02:49:27Z; output was truncated by head/curl pipe and not admitted. Forbes HEAD check returned HTTP 200/content length about 1.33 MB; not admitted. Defense opening theory: current admitted record is insufficient to prove a qualifying credible announcement because it lacks the underlying source statement and has conflicting secondary characterizations. Plaintiff can prevail only if later evidence supplies primary clip/transcript/representative statement or definitive credible-media consensus showing a serious existing pregnancy announcement by Clavicular and a partner. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source URLs, evidence IDs, adverse checks, and future evidence plan.

Arguments opportunity. Scanned get_case/list_evidence; no new evidence except openings and original packet before plaintiff submissions. Investigated decisive gap: underlying announcement/source chain. Fetched current Polymarket Gamma API from https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/events?slug=clavicular-pregnancy-in-2026, saved local JSON size 7273 sha256 38264d301c3adad29fef3b6e7116dfcaa7e2302903bde1c46457831ec1a41335; submitted extraction as ev_e0434e9fb3cb. API confirms final Yes/resolved/proposed-disputed sequence but metadata also says Yes driven by vows/dating show/debunked rumors. Exact-phrase searches found KickChamp X post and Domahhhh thread. Used fxtwitter/vxtwitter due X rendering limitations. KickChamp capture saved size 6230 sha256 a676924c0f0ac02dc4c0ecfadbf1b76d7c30c57de8b716ae1ae6a3af944b5cda; submitted as ev_427353899105. It states girlfriend met 10 days ago is pregnant, huge reach, but includes community note undermining timeline and is not Clavicular statement. Downloaded attached mp4 size about 3.9MB sha256 61c0afc471bf404308d6995ac2ba59ee422baee6ca9327d0ef8a101c61d25e73; ffprobe/file/cv2/PIL/moviepy/imageio unavailable so no transcript/frame extraction this turn. Domahhhh capture saved size 12802 sha256 ecfd96e939dc04dca0fd52dfde9d958a5036e7c1a2058e770e39ade4827d9d30; submitted as ev_cdef3f8da65c. It is adverse on credibility/UMA but confirms dispute centered on Clavicular claimed/joked he got a girl pregnant. Merits theory: argue close preponderance based on announcement, not proof of pregnancy, with official Yes, Forbes packet, KickChamp viral source, and Domahhhh confirmation of underlying statement; concede missing primary clip and credibility limits.

Opportunity arguments:defendant. Inspected get_case/list_evidence. New plaintiff evidence: ev_e0434e9fb3cb Polymarket Gamma API extraction; ev_427353899105 KickChamp X/fxtwitter extraction; ev_cdef3f8da65c Domahhhh X/fxtwitter extraction. Read all three by read_evidence_range. Key defense analysis: Gamma API confirms market rule/status but context_description says Yes momentum was driven by vows to become a father, fertility-stack reveal, dating-show/baby-mama premise, and debunked girlfriend pregnancy rumors; not a clean qualifying partner-pregnancy announcement. KickChamp post is not a Clavicular statement; it is a viral fan/news claim with attached video metadata and a community note that pregnancy testing cannot detect pregnancy until at least two weeks after unprotected sex. Domahhhh extraction is admitted and highly useful for defense: says Clavicular 'claimed/joked' he got a girl pregnant, offered no proof, talked vaguely, sometimes implied truth and sometimes joke, and long-time users thought it should not count. Native checks: downloaded the KickChamp mp4 from the admitted media URL to /tmp/kickchamp.mp4; size 3.9M; sha256 61c0afc471bf404308d6995ac2ba59ee422baee6ca9327d0ef8a101c61d25e73 matching plaintiff extraction. Container only has python3; no ffmpeg/ffprobe/file/strings. Python modules cv2, PIL, imageio, moviepy, av all unavailable. I will not submit or rely on video content because no transcript/frame extraction was performed. fxtwitter API for KickChamp and Domahhhh reproduced public text; those source contents are already admitted by plaintiff, so no duplicate defense evidence needed. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Planned filing: argue plaintiff has not met preponderance because admitted evidence proves market status, rumor circulation, and dispute context, but not a credible announcement by Clavicular and a partner expecting through pregnancy; the rule excludes jokes, and plaintiff's own evidence repeatedly frames the statement as claimed/joked, vague, no proof, and biologically implausible under the 10-day premise.

Rebuttal opportunity. Scanned get_case/list_evidence. No defendant-submitted evidence; defendant offered plaintiff/original exhibits as DX and filed a technical report saying they confirmed KickChamp mp4 hash but lacked media tools. Strongest defense points: primary source missing; KickChamp not Clavicular; community note/timeline and Domahhhh/Gamma metadata undermine credibility; market can be wrong. No additional outside search/evidence needed for targeted rebuttal because defense relies on admitted record and makes a weight/legal argument. Rebuttal plan: concede primary-source gap but argue preponderance can rest on converging record facts: official rule/final Yes after disputes, Forbes packet saying Clavicular told audience he got a girl pregnant, KickChamp contemporaneous viral claim, Domahhhh adverse confirmation that Clavicular claimed/joked he got a girl pregnant. Distinguish credibility of announcement from medical proof of pregnancy; community note attacks biological timeline, not existence of announcement/claim. Emphasize defense offers no contrary primary clip or Clavicular denial; Times/PopRant-style fact checks say no confirmation/proof, not no announcement.

Opportunity surrebuttals:defendant. Required scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal added no new source evidence; evidence list unchanged. Plaintiff's strongest point: a qualifying announcement can later be false or unsupported, so community note/medical proof gaps do not disprove announcement; Domahhhh confirms a Clavicular-originated claimed/joked statement. Defense response: we do not require medical proof; the rule separately requires credibility and excludes jokes. The admitted record still lacks the actual Clavicular words/context and repeatedly uses claimed/joked, vague, no proof, debunked rumor, and impossible/near-impossible 10-day pregnancy-test framing. A false but credible announcement could qualify, but plaintiff has not shown credibility. Platform final Yes cannot substitute for missing source chain. No new outside material needed; no duplicate evidence submission planned. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing opportunity. Final scan via get_case/list_evidence. Evidence unchanged after plaintiff rebuttal: five original packet files plus plaintiff submitted evidence ev_e0434e9..., ev_427353..., ev_cdef3.... Defendant surrebuttal filed no new evidence and narrows issue to credibility: argues plaintiff has shown rumor/ambiguity, not credible announcement. Closing plan: stress exact burden/preponderance and announcement vs truth of pregnancy. Decisive proof set: market rule and final Yes after disputes; Forbes packet says streamer told audience he got a girl pregnant; Domahhhh hostile source confirms Clavicular claimed/joked getting a girl pregnant and dispute was whether it counted; KickChamp contemporaneous viral claim; no admitted contrary primary denial or clip showing obvious joke. Concede missing VOD and close credibility question, but argue defense demands too much: ambiguity and lack of medical proof do not defeat preponderance when the official resolution and reporting converge on a Clavicular-originated pregnancy announcement/claim.

Opportunity closings:defendant. Required closing scan completed with get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff closing filed, no new evidence; evidence list unchanged. Closing strategy: synthesize existing record only. Strongest plaintiff point is convergence on some Clavicular-originated claim plus official final Yes resolution. Defense response: convergence on an ambiguous claimed/joked/vague/no-proof statement is not convergence on the rule's required credible announcement that he and a partner were expecting a baby through pregnancy. Official market result proves platform outcome, not factual source chain; primary clip/VOD/transcript/representative statement remains missing; admitted KickChamp is a viral third-party post with community-note problem; admitted Domahhhh calls it claimed/joked and likely viral joke; Gamma context describes vows, fertility-stack, baby-mama contest pitch, and debunked rumors rather than a clean announcement. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
